stories.headline: Xiaomi MiMo-V2-Pro: 1-trillion-parameter agent model that ran stealth as Hunter Alpha now ranks #8 worldwide
stories.summary: Xiaomi revealed MiMo-V2-Pro on March 19, a 1T parameter MoE model with 42B active parameters and a 1M token context window, secretly deployed on OpenRouter as Hunter Alpha processing over 1 trillion tokens before anyone knew it was Xiaomi's. At $0.40/M input tokens it ranks #8 globally on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index and approaches Claude Opus 4.6 performance on agentic benchmarks at one-sixth the cost.
stories.lateral_explanation: Cephalopods evolved the ability to mask their true size and species, accumulating intelligence about their environment under a false appearance before revealing themselves. MiMo-V2-Pro's Hunter Alpha stealth deployment follows the same strategy: 1 trillion tokens of real production traffic validated

stories.headline: Okta for AI Agents GA April 30: agents registered as first-class identities across 8,200+ enterprise integrations
stories.summary: Okta announced its Okta for AI Agents platform, GA April 30, which registers every AI agent as an independent identity with its own lifecycle rather than allowing agents to hide behind shared human service accounts. The move addresses a critical gap Okta's own research exposed: 88% of organizations report suspected or confirmed AI agent security incidents, yet only 22% treat agents as distinct identities.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to medieval guild charter law
stories.lateral_explanation: In medieval Europe, new classes of power-holders had to be formally recognized as distinct legal entities before they could participate in institutional governance. Okta's problem is structurally identical: AI agents are new actors operating inside institutional systems, but without formal recogniti

stories.headline: Entro Security launches Agentic Governance and Administration: map every AI agent's identity, permissions, and MCP connections
stories.summary: Entro Security's AGA platform, released March 18, builds a structured profile for every enterprise AI agent by correlating three data layers: where the agent runs, what it accesses, and which identities it uses, with native integration into AWS Bedrock, Copilot Studio, and MCP servers. Unlike credential-scanning tools, AGA enforces policy as agents execute, making it the first platform to govern what an agent actually does rather than merely that it exists.
stories.lateral_explanation: Entomologists studying ant colonies developed individual tagging techniques to map which workers access which resources, because collective behavior was invisible without individual tracking. Entro's AGA applies the same logic to AI agents: without tagging each agent with a structured identity profi

stories.headline: BeyondTrust Phantom Labs: enterprise AI agents grew 466.7% year-over-year, many with administrator-level privileges
stories.summary: BeyondTrust's Phantom Labs released data on March 23 showing a 466.7% year-over-year increase in AI agents inside enterprise environments, with many organizations running over 1,000 agents that security teams were not fully aware of, several carrying admin-level privileges. The core risk is structural: agents can inherit permissions from human accounts and act autonomously across systems in ways traditional privileged access management tools cannot detect.
stories.lateral_explanation: Cosmologists discovered dark matter not by observing it directly but by noticing gravitational effects on visible matter that could not be explained by the mass they could see. BeyondTrust's finding is structurally identical: enterprise IT systems are behaving in ways that cannot be explained by the

stories.headline: Keycard and Smallstep anchor AI agent runtime security to verified hardware: credentials that cannot leave their authorized machine
stories.summary: Keycard and Smallstep released a joint solution that cryptographically binds AI agent credentials to specific hardware, making it impossible to reuse or impersonate them from a different environment. The approach applies hardware-root-of-trust PKI principles to the AI agent runtime problem, treating agent identity the way enterprise security treats TLS certificates: bound to the infrastructure, not the software.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Venetian Murano glass secrets
stories.lateral_explanation: Venice maintained its glassblowing monopoly for centuries by physically binding knowledge to location: only workers resident on the island of Murano could practice the craft, and the secrets were tied to the place itself rather than any portable document. Keycard's approach is the cryptographic equi

stories.headline: Microsoft Agent 365: enterprise control plane for AI agent fleets integrating Defender, Entra, and Purview, GA May 1
stories.summary: Microsoft announced Agent 365 on March 20, a unified governance layer giving IT and security teams a single interface to observe, govern, and revoke access for every AI agent across the enterprise, with automated registration, per-agent access controls, PII detection toggles, and full audit trails. GA on May 1, the platform completes Microsoft's end-to-end agentic stack: model layer (Azure OpenAI), execution layer (Copilot Studio), and governance layer (Agent 365) as an integrated suite.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to air traffic control invention
stories.lateral_explanation: Centralized air traffic control in the 1950s-60s solved the same problem Agent 365 addresses: many autonomous vehicles operating in shared airspace without any single plane knowing what all others were doing. The solution required a separate layer not embedded in the aircraft that could see all acto

stories.headline: NIST launches AI Agent Standards Initiative: federal body opens industry input for agent interoperability and identity frameworks
stories.summary: The US National Institute of Standards and Technology opened a formal industry input process for developing AI agent standards covering identity management, permission models, audit logging, and cross-platform agent handoffs. NIST's involvement signals the transition from voluntary enterprise policy to regulated infrastructure, with standards expected to inform federal procurement requirements that historically propagate into private-sector compliance within 12-24 months.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to AC/DC electrical standards war
stories.lateral_explanation: The winner of the AC vs DC power competition was not decided by technical superiority alone but by which standard got written into building codes and municipal contracts. NIST's AI Agent Standards Initiative is the opening move in the same game: the organizations that shape the interoperability spec

stories.headline: OpenAI crosses $25B in annualized revenue: fastest revenue growth in technology history, 9 million paying businesses
stories.summary: OpenAI reached $25 billion in annualized revenue by end of February 2026, growing 17% in two months from $21.4B at year-end 2025, with paying businesses doubling from 5M to 9M in six months. CEO Sam Altman is steering toward an IPO targeting a $1 trillion valuation, with a potential filing in H2 2026.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Dutch East India Company IPO
stories.lateral_explanation: The VOC's 1602 IPO was the first time the public could invest in a company operating autonomous agents at global scale, with captains and factors making binding decisions thousands of miles from oversight. The mechanism that made it work was contractual standardization: uniform accounting converted

stories.headline: Nudge Security releases MCP server monitoring and shadow AI discovery tools for agentic enterprise environments
stories.summary: Nudge Security expanded its platform with tools that continuously track MCP server connections, agentic workflow integrations, and OAuth links between AI tools and enterprise data, providing a live inventory of every AI agent created inside an organization including those built outside IT visibility. The platform detects when sensitive data is passed through AI prompts and maps the full graph of SaaS-to-AI integrations.
stories.lateral_explanation: Ecologists managing invasive species developed passive continuous tracking because periodic manual surveys consistently miss the early spread window when management is still feasible. Nudge Security's lightweight IdP integration that continuously logs every AI account creation and MCP connection is

stories.headline: Accenture and Microsoft Forward Deployed Engineering: thousands of AI engineers embedded directly inside enterprise client environments
stories.summary: Accenture and Microsoft launched a Forward Deployed Engineering practice on March 18, embedding thousands of AI-skilled engineers from both organizations directly inside client organizations to move stalled AI initiatives from pilot to production. The structure mirrors Palantir's government deployment model: technical experts inside the client environment rather than consulting from outside.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to medieval craft guild apprenticeship
stories.lateral_explanation: Medieval craft guilds solved technology transfer by embedding skilled practitioners directly inside the production environment of adopting organizations, because complex tacit knowledge cannot be transferred through manuals or external workshops alone. The FDE model is structurally identical: how to

stories.headline: Confirm launches unified AI HR Agents Platform at Transform 2026: autonomous HRBP, onboarding, coaching, and service desk
stories.summary: Confirm debuted its Unified AI Agents HR Platform at Transform 2026 (March 23-25), shipping four production-grade autonomous HR agents: an AI HRBP surfacing retention risks using organizational network analysis, an onboarding agent guiding 90-day journeys, a manager coaching agent delivering real-time growth plans, and a service desk agent resolving employee questions in Slack and Teams without ticket queues. At $8 per person per month the platform replaces the sprawl of single-purpose HR AI tools that most enterprises have accumulated without measurable ROI.
stories.lateral_explanation: Frederick Taylor's scientific management decomposed complex work into measurable sub-tasks and created the entire management function of HR as a system for routing and coaching human workers. Confirm's AI HRBP, coaching, and service desk agents are the logical completion of the Taylor project: if th

stories.headline: 72% of Global 2000 companies now run AI agents beyond pilot stage, with multi-agent orchestration as the default architecture
stories.summary: Enterprise adoption data shows 72% of Global 2000 companies operate AI agent systems beyond experimental testing, with multi-agent orchestration emerging as the dominant production architecture for workflows too complex for single-agent handling. Telecommunications and retail sectors lead at 47-48% adoption, driven by the operational volume where agents produce measurable cost and speed advantages over both human workflows and earlier RPA automation.
stories.lateral_explanation: Epidemiologists define an epidemic threshold as the point at which a pathogen has enough hosts to sustain transmission without dying out. At 72% adoption among Global 2000 companies, AI agents have crossed the equivalent threshold in enterprise technology: the capability is no longer a competitive d

stories.headline: BeyondTrust: enterprises running 1,000+ AI agents they did not fully know existed, many with admin-level access to critical systems
stories.summary: BeyondTrust Phantom Labs data reveals that many large enterprises now operate over 1,000 AI agents created outside IT governance, carrying permissions comparable to human administrators able to read databases, call APIs, and modify enterprise systems without any human in the approval chain. The pattern directly mirrors the shadow IT crisis of the 2010s but operates at higher speed and higher privilege: where shadow IT created unauthorized tools, shadow AI creates unauthorized autonomous workers.
stories.lateral_explanation: Medieval mercenary companies were initially contracted for specific campaigns but gradually accumulated their own equipment, intelligence, and operational independence. Some employers discovered their contractors had more effective fighting capacity and information advantage than the armies that hir

stories.headline: OpenAI $25B vs Anthropic $19B: the AI revenue race confirms enterprise AI workers have crossed from innovation budget to operational line item
stories.summary: The gap between OpenAI ($25B ARR) and Anthropic ($19B ARR) has narrowed sharply in Q1 2026, with both companies growing faster than any enterprise software business in history in a competitive race that is forcing accelerated agentic feature shipping. The trajectory confirms that AI as a workforce tool has crossed from R&D budget to operational line item: companies are paying for AI workers the way they pay for SaaS licenses, not as one-time research projects.
stories.lateral_explanation: The economic value created by the printing press took roughly 50 years to fully manifest in new industries that did not exist before the technology arrived. The OpenAI/Anthropic revenue race is in its early printing press phase: the numbers are extraordinary but the deeper structural transformation

stories.headline: Accenture and Microsoft Forward Deployed Engineering: largest professional services bet on moving enterprise AI from pilot to production
stories.summary: Accenture and Microsoft's joint FDE practice pairs thousands of AI engineers from both organizations to work directly inside client environments, addressing the dominant reason enterprise AI initiatives stall: not the technology but insufficient engineering expertise applied at the right point in the organization's own processes. The structure is the first time two of the three largest enterprise technology players have jointly committed headcount to the last mile of enterprise AI deployment.
stories.lateral_explanation: The transcontinental railroad was built not by central planning organizations but by specialized end-of-track construction gangs who moved with the frontier, carrying tacit knowledge that no manual could describe. AI deployment does not fail at the model layer but at the junction between AI capabili

stories.headline: Okta research: 88% of organizations experienced AI agent security incidents, most because agents inherit human admin permissions
stories.summary: Okta's research shows 88% of organizations report suspected or confirmed AI agent security incidents, with the root cause traced to 78% of enterprises allowing agents to inherit permissions from human accounts rather than having distinct governance. As agents become de facto employees handling sensitive data, the identity governance gap is the primary unsolved operational risk in enterprise AI deployment.
stories.lateral_explanation: Sociologist James C. Scott's concept of legibility describes how states developed standardized identities not to benefit citizens but to make populations visible to governance. Okta's finding is precisely the legibility failure Scott described: the enterprise cannot audit, constrain, or revoke what

stories.headline: Microsoft Agent 365 GA May 1: IT gets a control plane for governing AI agent fleets at enterprise scale
stories.summary: Microsoft Agent 365 will be generally available on May 1, providing IT and security teams a unified interface for registering, monitoring, and revoking access for every AI agent across the enterprise with Defender, Entra, and Purview integration. The announcement positions Microsoft as the only vendor with a governance layer spanning the full agent lifecycle: creation, identity, runtime monitoring, data access, and decommissioning.
stories.lateral_explanation: Central banks emerged not to create money but to provide a regulatory layer that could see all money-creating actors simultaneously and constrain their behavior relative to each other. Agent 365 is the central banking moment for enterprise AI agents: individual platforms will continue creating agent

stories.headline: ServiceNow and NVIDIA ship Autonomous Workforce Specialists: named enterprise deployments handling ITSM, HR, and procurement
stories.summary: ServiceNow's Autonomous Workforce of AI Specialists, built with NVIDIA Agent Toolkit software, entered production at named enterprise clients with AI specialists autonomously handling ITSM ticket triage, HR case routing, and procurement workflows inside existing ServiceNow environments. The integration positions NVIDIA's Agent Toolkit as the common substrate beneath every major enterprise AI workforce platform, from ServiceNow to SAP Joule to Salesforce Agentforce.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Ford assembly line logic
stories.lateral_explanation: Henry Ford invented the modular assembly line that made production independent of any single worker's skill by decomposing processes into standardized steps any worker could perform reliably. ServiceNow's Autonomous Workforce Specialists follow the same logic: they decompose enterprise workflows int

stories.headline: NIST AI Agent Standards Initiative signals that enterprise AI worker governance is transitioning from voluntary to regulated
stories.summary: NIST's AI Agent Standards Initiative opens a formal industry input process for interoperability, identity management, and audit logging standards covering the governance gap between what enterprise AI agents can do and what any governing body can enforce. Standards developed here are expected to inform federal procurement requirements that historically propagate into private-sector compliance frameworks within 12-24 months.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to maritime law of the sea
stories.lateral_explanation: The Law of the Sea evolved to address the same governance challenge: autonomous vehicles operating across jurisdictions accumulating real-world consequences without any single authority having complete visibility. The foundational UNCLOS principle, that the flag state bears governance responsibility

stories.headline: OpenAI rolls out ads to free tier users: $1B projected 2026 revenue will fund AI work access for organizations that cannot pay enterprise prices
stories.summary: OpenAI began serving advertisements to free and entry-tier ChatGPT users in January 2026, projecting $1 billion in ad revenue this year scaling to $25 billion by 2029, creating an ad-supported AI work access model that mirrors how social media democratized publishing. The model directly addresses the 90% of organizations below the Global 2000 that have not yet deployed production AI agents due to enterprise licensing costs.
stories.lateral_explanation: Andrew Carnegie's public library campaign was funded not primarily as philanthropy but as infrastructure investment: literate workers were more productive, and free access via a third-party funding model accelerated literacy adoption faster than market pricing could. OpenAI's ad-supported model is s

stories.headline: Dia Mundial del Teatro el 27 de marzo en Madrid: La vida extraordinaria en los Teatros del Canal con coloquio abierto y visitas gratuitas
stories.summary: Los Teatros del Canal celebran el Dia Mundial del Teatro (27 de marzo) con la funcion de La vida extraordinaria de Mariano Tenconi Blanco, protagonizada por Malena Alterio y Carmen Ruiz, seguida de un coloquio publico y descuento del 25% en entradas, mientras ofrecen visitas guiadas gratuitas a espacios habitualmente vedados al publico. Ese mismo dia el Corral de Comedias de Alcala de Henares presenta La hoguera de las maravillas, basada en textos del Siglo de Oro: el teatro mas vivo de la capital y el mas antiguo de la region compartiendo cartelera.
stories.lateral_explanation: Greek theatre did not emerge as entertainment but as civic ritual: the festival of Dionysus was a structured civic obligation in Athens, with full attendance expected as democratic participation. The Dia Mundial del Teatro's combination of free tours, discounted tickets, and public discussion follow

stories.headline: Exposicion Valle-Inclan en Madrid en la Biblioteca Regional: el esperpento como metodo de ver la ciudad, hasta el 21 de junio
stories.summary: La Biblioteca Regional de Madrid reune por primera vez manuscritos, primeras ediciones, fotografias y correspondencia que trazan la relacion entre Valle-Inclan y Madrid, la ciudad donde invento el esperpento como distorsion de la realidad para describir la decadencia. El 31 de marzo el curador Joaquin del Valle-Inclan Alsina ofrece una conferencia publica que plantea la pregunta central de la exposicion: sigue siendo el esperpento el marco mas honesto para leer Madrid?
stories.lateral_explanation: The Situationist International developed psychogeography in 1950s Paris to describe how the urban environment generates distinct emotional and cognitive states in those who move through it. Valle-Inclan was practicing psychogeography before the term existed: Luces de Bohemia maps Madrid not as a cit

stories.headline: Teatralia cierra su 30a edicion el 29 de marzo: 30 anos de artes escenicas internacionales para todos los publicos en 17 municipios
stories.summary: El Festival Internacional de Artes Escenicas Teatralia cierra el 29 de marzo su 30a edicion con 29 companias de 11 paises y 30 espectaculos que combinan teatro gestual, circo, danza, musica en directo y teatro de objetos, distribuidos en diecisiete municipios de la Comunidad de Madrid. La ultima semana incluye funciones en La Casa Encendida, el Teatro Pradillo, el Replika Teatro y el Teatro Circo Price, con funcion de tarde el miercoles 25 para publico general.
stories.lateral_explanation: The medieval traveling circus and mystery play circuit was not a primitive precursor to settled theatre but a distinct art form that required audiences to understand theatre as a temporary irruption of the extraordinary into the ordinary. Teatralia's distribution across seventeen municipalities foll

stories.headline: Cartas de mujeres en el Teatro Espanol: correspondencia interrumpida de distintas epocas en escena hasta el 12 de abril
stories.summary: El Teatro Espanol presenta hasta el 12 de abril Cartas de mujeres, una pieza construida sobre cartas no enviadas escritas por mujeres de distintas epocas que reflexiona sobre la amistad, el cuidado, la perdida y la intimidad, el genero epistolar como archivo de lo que no pudo decirse en voz alta. La coincidencia con la Semana Santa convierte al Espanol en un espacio de recogimiento laico: el mismo impulso de introspection que mueve a la ciudad hacia las procesiones encuentra aqui su equivalente secular.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to dead letter office archives
stories.lateral_explanation: The dead letter office, created by postal services from the 18th century onward, was a repository of lives in suspension: letters written with full intention of delivery that never arrived. Historians have used these archives as primary sources for social history precisely because unsent letters are

stories.headline: V Festival de Zarzuela en el Teatro Amaya: el genero que Madrid invento regresa como formato vivo hasta el 31 de mayo
stories.summary: El Teatro Amaya acoge hasta el 31 de mayo la quinta edicion del Festival de Zarzuela, el unico ciclo monografico de Madrid dedicado a la zarzuela como formato activo y no como patrimonio en vitrina. La zarzuela nacio en el Madrid del siglo XVII en el Buen Retiro como entretenimiento cortesano, se convirtio en el XIX en el genero popular que definio la identidad musical de la ciudad frente a la opera italiana, y hoy sobrevive como la forma musical mas genuinamente madrilena.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Victorian Music Hall tradition
stories.lateral_explanation: The Victorian Music Hall emerged in 1840s London from pub entertainment and competed directly with opera for working-class audiences, combining music, comedy, and social critique in the vernacular language and being attacked by defenders of high culture. The Festival de Zarzuela's claim that the for

stories.headline: Luces de Bohemia y Ulysses de Joyce: conferencia en la Biblioteca Regional conecta las dos grandes novelas de ciudades en declive
stories.summary: La programacion de la exposicion Valle-Inclan incluye una conferencia de Dario Villanueva trazando los paralelos entre el Madrid de Luces de Bohemia (1920) y el Dublin de Ulysses (1922), dos obras del mismo ano escritas desde el mismo diagnostico: una ciudad que ha dejado de creer en si misma, habitada por personajes que cargan con toda la grandeza y toda la miseria de una cultura en transito. El paralelo entre Max Estrella y Leopold Bloom como arquetipos del intelectual europeo del XX en crisis es una de las conexiones literarias mas subestimadas del canon moderno.
stories.lateral_explanation: Walter Benjamin's Arcades Project was an attempt to write the mythology of 19th-century Paris through its material culture, the passages and street types that condensed the city's contradictions into legible images. Both Luces de Bohemia and Ulysses were doing the same for Madrid and Dublin: constru

books.title: Co-Intelligence: Living and Working with AI
books.author: Ethan Mollick
books.why: Mollick is the most evidence-based writer on how real professionals change how they work with AI. After a week of stories about Confirm HR agents, BeyondTrust shadow AI workforces, and 72% of Global 2000 companies running agents in production, this book gives the conceptual framework for what working with AI actually means at the individual level versus the enterprise infrastructure level.

books.title: AI Engineering: Building Applications with Foundation Models
books.author: Chip Huyen
books.why: Huyen wrote the definitive guide to building production AI applications. This week's stories about Entro AGA's multi-layer agent profiling, Okta for AI Agents' identity governance, and Microsoft Agent 365's control plane all point to the same theme: AI engineering is now as much about governance and identity as about model selection. Huyen's framework for evaluation, deployment, and reliability is the missing technical context.
